topics.description: Where the suspect was at a specific time.
claim_templates: I was on the terrace the whole time, nowhere near the study.
I never left the drawing room before the toast.
I was upstairs, alone, from nine until the commotion started.
I was in the garden getting air; ask anyone.
contradiction_templates: A guest recalls seeing {speaker} near the study at nine, not on the terrace as claimed.
The drawing room's other occupants never saw {speaker} enter before the toast.
Staff confirm {speaker} was downstairs, not upstairs, during the window in question.
Nobody in the garden remembers {speaker} being there at all.

topics.description: The nature of the suspect's relationship to the victim or another suspect.
claim_templates: I had no real business with them.
We had not spoken in years.
contradiction_templates: A letter in {speaker}'s hand shows a far closer and more strained tie to the victim than admitted.
A ledger entry ties {speaker} to the victim's affairs well beyond mere friendliness.
Correspondence found on the premises shows {speaker} and the victim in regular, pointed contact.
A signed record proves {speaker} and the victim spoke as recently as last week.

topics.description: What the suspect saw, heard, or noticed.
claim_templates: I didn't see anything unusual all evening.
I noticed nothing out of place near the study.
I heard nothing before the commotion.
Nothing seemed wrong to me at the time.
contradiction_templates: Another guest recalls {speaker} reacting sharply to something near the study, well before the alarm was raised.
A member of staff remembers {speaker} remarking on the broken latch hours before anyone else noticed it.
{speaker} was overheard describing a sound from the study that {speaker} now denies having heard.
A second witness places {speaker} close enough to have seen exactly what {speaker} claims not to have seen.

topics.description: Whether the suspect had, held, or owned a particular thing.
claim_templates: I had nothing to do with that item.
That was never in my possession.
I never touched it, not once.
It was not mine to begin with.
contradiction_templates: A found receipt ties the item directly to {speaker}, contradicting the denial.
A staff member recalls handing the item to {speaker} personally earlier that evening.
{speaker}'s initials are engraved on the very item {speaker} claims never to have touched.
The item was recovered from a room only {speaker} had access to that night.
